metadata.title: From Commits to Cognition: A Mixed-Methods Framework for Inferring Developer Mental Models from Repository Artifacts
metadata.description:
Understanding how software developers think remains a fundamental challenge in empirical software engineering. While previous research has examined developer behavior through surveys, interviews, and controlled experiments, few studies have attempted to reverse-engineer cognitive patterns directly from repository artifacts.

This paper presents a pilot study combining Mining Software Repositories (MSR), Reflexive Thematic Analysis, and Autoethnography to infer developer mental models from commits, code structure, documentation, and project organization. Through a longitudinal self-study analyzing 40 repositories and 2,799 commits over approximately three years (2022–2025), we identify four dimensions of developer cognition: cognitive, affective, conative, and reflective.

The resulting Developer Mental Model Framework (DMMF) provides a replicable protocol for developer self-analysis. We present quantitative analyses of commit patterns, BERT-based sentiment classification, and temporal clustering of refactor sequences alongside qualitative themes from reflexive analysis.

As a single-subject exploratory study, our primary contribution is methodological: demonstrating the feasibility of inferring cognitive patterns from repository artifacts.
metadata.notes: v1.2.0 (2026-05-08): adopt NTLabs.dev canonical preprint template (single-column, monochrome). Source LaTeX reconstructed from the v1.0 PDF deposit (the original Overleaf source was not preserved locally) and committed to the repository for future versioning at paper/main.tex. Corrected metadata errors from v1.0: ORCID was 0000-0002-5139-0980 (incorrect, not the author's), now 0009-0001-7144-4974 (verified via Lattes); affiliation was UFRJ (incorrect, the author was never affiliated with UFRJ), now NTLabs.dev / IFSULDEMINAS. Added institutional email (anderson@ntlabs.dev). GitHub repository URL updated from anderson-ufrj to anderson-ntlabs after account rename. No changes to scientific content or empirical findings.
